★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 24.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.10 3.84 3.11 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.19 0.67 0.19 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.85 0.85 0.86 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.06 2.32 2.07 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.04 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.15 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.61
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.15
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.62
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

annual

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.36
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio

facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-08-01 19 7 15 152 1 152
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-10-24 13 11 2 76 2 114
Total weighted health score
142.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (44)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

43 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. E 2025-12-18 2026-01-09
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. E 2025-11-24 2025-12-05
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-11-24 2025-12-05
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. E 2025-11-21 2026-01-09
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-09-10 2025-11-26
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2025-07-09 2025-07-11
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2025-05-16 2025-07-11
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2025-05-16 2025-06-27
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-05-16 2025-06-27
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-03-17 2025-04-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-01-17 2025-03-03
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-01-17 2025-03-03
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2024-08-01 2024-09-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2022-10-24 2023-01-11
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2022-10-24 2023-01-11
0776 Provide timely, approved x-ray services, or have an agreement with an approved provider to obtain them. D 2022-10-24 2023-01-11
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2022-10-24 2022-12-08
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. E 2019-11-07 2019-12-22
